title: Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
summary: PostHog is building an automated pipeline that detects product signals like rage clicks and error spikes, groups them via an LLM agent, retrieves relevant session replays and codebase context, and automatically generates pull requests with fixes. The system collapses the traditional triage and development workflow into an autonomous agent that produces ready-to-merge PRs.
writeup: Joshua Snyder of PostHog details an automated pipeline that turns product signals into pull requests. The system detects signals like rage clicks and error spikes, groups them with an LLM agent, retrieves relevant session replays and codebase context, and generates fixes as ready-to-merge PRs. It collapses the traditional triage and development workflow into a single autonomous agent. The talk covers the supporting pieces: clustering to group signals, LLM evaluation, error recovery, and test-time compute.
